drift_title: Older non-JP/SG/AU Amazon orders silently age out of Orders API responses
silent_success_surface: Order search/list calls can return 200 with no matching orders for records older than the supported retention window.
behavior_change: Historical backfills can miss orders older than the marketplace retention window while the API response remains valid.
offline_blocker: Requires live seller order history older than the marketplace retention window.
why_not_hard_break: The endpoint returns a valid response; missing data is a retention/visibility rule.
notes: New candidate; counted toward 78 new L3+ rows.

drift_title: Migrated Amazon Orders responses omit subtrees unless includedData requests them
silent_success_surface: searchOrders/getOrder succeeds while buyer, recipient, proceeds, package, or fulfillment sections can be absent.
behavior_change: Migrated syncs can under-collect order fields unless they request the new includedData sections.
offline_blocker: Requires live migration comparison on real seller orders.
why_not_hard_break: The request is valid; omission is controlled by request semantics rather than an error.
notes: Distinct from existing OrderItemId mismatch case.

drift_title: Shopify Order resource exposes only the last 60 days by default
silent_success_surface: Order list/count calls return 200 and can look complete while older orders are inaccessible without read_all_orders approval.
behavior_change: Backfills and reconciliation jobs using default access silently miss older orders.
offline_blocker: Requires a shop with older orders and app approval/scope state.
why_not_hard_break: The endpoint works and returns valid pages; the default access window limits visibility.
notes: Distinct from existing Shopify versioning and protected customer data cases.

api_surface: Admin REST cancel order / refund behavior
drift_title: Manual-gateway refunds can be recorded without refunding the customer
silent_success_surface: Cancel/refund flow can return 200 and create refund records while no money moves for manual-gateway original transactions.
behavior_change: Systems treating recorded refund state as settlement can overstate customer refunds.
offline_blocker: Needs a live manual-gateway order and payment-provider settlement observation.
why_not_hard_break: Shopify records the refund as designed; financial completion is outside that gateway path.

drift_title: Orders can contain multiple delivery methods requiring fulfillment-order interpretation
silent_success_surface: Order reads succeed, but order-level delivery assumptions can assign the wrong fulfillment method or location.
behavior_change: Split carts and fulfillment-order workflows make a single order unsafe to interpret as one delivery path.
offline_blocker: Requires live orders with split delivery methods or multiple fulfillment orders.
why_not_hard_break: Order and fulfillment APIs return valid objects; the drift is semantic granularity.

drift_title: REST orderId and legacyOrderId are not interchangeable across eBay API families
silent_success_surface: Fulfillment API returns both IDs successfully, but downstream legacy calls require the compatible identifier.
behavior_change: Cross-API order processing can silently use the wrong identifier surface after reading a valid order object.
offline_blocker: Requires live cross-API order processing against Sell Fulfillment and legacy APIs.
why_not_hard_break: The read succeeds and both IDs are present; the drift is identifier selection.
notes: Distinct from existing eBay feed partial success case.

drift_title: Order status custom labels can drift from static status IDs
silent_success_surface: Order/status APIs return 200 with merchant-customized labels.
behavior_change: Integrations matching labels instead of status IDs can silently bucket orders incorrectly.
offline_blocker: Requires a live store where merchants customized order status labels.
why_not_hard_break: The status response is valid; display labels are mutable by design.

drift_title: Deleting a shipment does not move the order out of shipped status
silent_success_surface: Shipment delete/update workflows succeed while order-level status remains shipped.
behavior_change: Fulfillment reconciliation can diverge when it treats shipment existence as equivalent to shipped status.
offline_blocker: Requires live order shipment creation, status change, and deletion.
why_not_hard_break: Shipment operations are valid; order status persistence is documented behavior.

api_surface: Orders V2 create/update order status and inventory
drift_title: Awaiting Fulfillment manual edits can leave inventory unadjusted
silent_success_surface: Order edit/update flows succeed while inventory quantities do not reflect stock changes.
behavior_change: Inventory reconciliation can drift after documented manual edits to Awaiting Fulfillment orders.
offline_blocker: Requires live store inventory tracking and manual order edits.
why_not_hard_break: The order edit succeeds; inventory divergence is a documented side effect.

drift_title: Cart purchases can appear as multiple orders unless pack_id is followed
silent_success_surface: Individual order retrieval succeeds but returns only that order's items, not the full purchase grouped in the pack.
behavior_change: Purchase-level operations can silently miss sibling orders unless the pack resource is queried.
offline_blocker: Requires live cart/pack orders with non-null pack_id.
why_not_hard_break: The order endpoint returns valid partial order data; the missing purchase-level view is a model distinction.

drift_title: PaymentIntent can sit in processing then later fail
silent_success_surface: Confirming a delayed-notification payment returns a PaymentIntent in processing after successful submission.
behavior_change: Fulfillment that treats processing as paid can run before Stripe later emits failure instead of success.
offline_blocker: Requires real delayed-notification rails and final network outcome timing.
why_not_hard_break: The API call succeeds and the object remains valid; only eventual business state changes.
notes: Distinct from existing Stripe webhook delivery case.

drift_title: Subscription can become active before async payment finality
silent_success_surface: Subscription creation can grant active access while the PaymentIntent is still processing.
behavior_change: If the async payment later fails, the invoice can be voided while subscription access has already been granted.
offline_blocker: Needs async payment method behavior and later settlement/failure event.
why_not_hard_break: No request fails; the documented lifecycle allows active subscription with later invoice failure.

drift_title: Pending refund reason exposes delayed failure causes
silent_success_surface: Refund API can create or show a refund with status pending rather than immediate finality.
behavior_change: Pending refunds can later fail due to insufficient funds, charge pending state, or disputed pending charge.
offline_blocker: Requires live balance, settlement, or dispute conditions around an in-flight refund.
why_not_hard_break: The refund request is accepted; final outcome arrives through status fields and events.

drift_title: Capture success true can still be rejected later by card scheme
silent_success_surface: CAPTURE webhook with success true indicates validations passed and the request was submitted.
behavior_change: The card scheme can still reject the capture later, producing CAPTURE_FAILED after apparent success.
offline_blocker: Requires card-scheme capture rejection or technical recapture workflow.
why_not_hard_break: Capture request and webhook are successful; later failure is a separate lifecycle event.

drift_title: Refund success true can later fail or reverse
silent_success_surface: REFUND webhook with success true indicates Adyen validations succeeded and refund was sent to the card scheme.
behavior_change: Card scheme rejection or reversal can later emit REFUND_FAILED or REFUNDED_REVERSED.
offline_blocker: Requires live scheme refund processing or reversal conditions.
why_not_hard_break: Initial refund validation succeeds; downstream scheme result changes final state.
notes: Refund analogue to Adyen capture drift.

drift_title: Remaining amount zero does not mean partial-payment order completed
silent_success_surface: Partial payments can reduce order.remainingAmount.value to 0 in the payment response.
behavior_change: Order completion depends on all payments reaching final status and ORDER_CLOSED success true, not merely remainingAmount 0.
offline_blocker: Requires multiple payment methods and final asynchronous status resolution.
why_not_hard_break: Responses are valid; intermediate aggregate state is not final business completion.
notes: Distinct from existing Adyen duplicate/latest webhook case.

api_surface: Payments API, ACH payments, payment.updated webhooks
drift_title: ACH payment created as PENDING later completes or fails
silent_success_surface: CreatePayment for ACH yields payment.created with status PENDING.
behavior_change: Square processes ACH asynchronously and later sends payment.updated with COMPLETED or FAILED.
offline_blocker: Requires Square ACH processing and bank outcome.
why_not_hard_break: Payment object is created successfully; final state changes asynchronously.
notes: Distinct from existing Square duplicate/out-of-order webhook case.

drift_title: RefundPayment may return PENDING before final failure or rejection
silent_success_surface: RefundPayment response can show status PENDING while Square processes funds movement.
behavior_change: Refund can later become FAILED or REJECTED because of seller balance, card, or processing conditions.
offline_blocker: Requires live seller balance/card/bank-transfer conditions and Square processing time.
why_not_hard_break: Refund request is accepted; failure is represented as later refund status.

drift_title: Captured payment can be PENDING or later FAILED instead of credited
silent_success_surface: Capture resource can exist with status PENDING after capture flow.
behavior_change: Funds may not be credited immediately and capture status can later become FAILED or DECLINED.
offline_blocker: Requires PayPal account state, eCheck/manual review/dispute, or funding rails.
why_not_hard_break: Capture API returns a documented non-final resource rather than rejecting the call.

drift_title: Processed payout can later reverse
silent_success_surface: Payout reaches processed, described as processed by the contact bank.
behavior_change: A processed payout can move to reversed if failure is detected later by bank or clearing-house reversal.
offline_blocker: Requires live bank/clearing-house reversal after payout processing.
why_not_hard_break: No endpoint failure occurs; terminal-looking business state mutates through documented reversal.
notes: Distinct from existing Razorpay webhook timeout case.

api_surface: Google Ads API ConversionUploadService / conversion reporting
drift_title: Successful click-conversion upload is not proof of attribution
silent_success_surface: UploadClickConversions returns a successful response or partial_failure only flags request/item errors.
behavior_change: Imported conversions can be accepted but later fail to attribute or appear after freshness delays under original click dates.
offline_blocker: Requires live clicks, matching signals, conversion customer ownership, and reporting freshness pipeline.
why_not_hard_break: The API call succeeds; the drift is downstream attribution/reporting semantics.
notes: Distinct from existing conversion adjustment partial failure case.

drift_title: Upload health moves to delayed aggregate diagnostics
silent_success_surface: Upload requests can succeed while later diagnostics show pending counts, successful counts, and alerts.
behavior_change: Immediate upload response does not fully describe final import health; diagnostics summarize later windows.
offline_blocker: Requires recent live uploads and Google diagnostics aggregation windows.
why_not_hard_break: Upload and diagnostics queries succeed; status interpretation is delayed and aggregated.

drift_title: Thresholded dimensions silently withhold rows or values
silent_success_surface: runReport succeeds but demographic or audience dimensions can be thresholded.
behavior_change: Privacy thresholds can suppress or reduce data for dimensions such as age, gender, interests, or audiences.
offline_blocker: Depends on live property user counts, signals, and Google-controlled privacy thresholds.
why_not_hard_break: The API returns reports; data is suppressed or reduced for privacy.

drift_title: Offline conversions can upload successfully but not count inside the conversion window
silent_success_surface: ApplyOfflineConversions returns tracking ID and no item-level partial error.
behavior_change: Conversions outside click/goal timing rules can be accepted yet not counted in downstream reporting.
offline_blocker: Requires live MSCLKID click history, goal settings, and Microsoft attribution processing.
why_not_hard_break: The operation can accept records; counting is a downstream attribution rule.

drift_title: Duplicate offline conversions are ignored under same click and conversion time
silent_success_surface: Upload operation can complete while duplicates do not increase conversions.
behavior_change: Duplicate offline conversions with same MicrosoftClickId and ConversionTime are ignored or collapsed under counting rules.
offline_blocker: Needs live goal CountType and click/conversion history to verify report impact.
why_not_hard_break: No request failure; dedup/counting semantics alter totals.

drift_title: VALID CAPI response does not guarantee match or attribution quality
silent_success_surface: Events endpoint returns status VALID and processed successfully.
behavior_change: Accepted events still need identity signals and signal readiness to match accounts and affect attribution.
offline_blocker: Requires live Snap accounts, event traffic, identity signals, and match-quality scoring.
why_not_hard_break: Event ingestion accepts the payload; downstream matching quality changes measurement results.

drift_title: Metrics-ready state gates finalized conversion reporting
silent_success_surface: Analytics endpoints can be queried before metrics are ready.
behavior_change: Readiness state determines whether conversion/non-conversion metrics are finalized and safe to query.
offline_blocker: Needs live Pinterest reporting jobs and freshness state.
why_not_hard_break: Reporting endpoints work; readiness changes whether results should be treated as final.

api_surface: Pinterest API v5 Ads Reporting conversion metrics
drift_title: Attribution windows and strongest-action attribution alter conversion totals
silent_success_surface: Ads reporting query succeeds with requested columns.
behavior_change: Attribution windows, conversion_report_time, and strongest-action rules can shift conversion totals without request failure.
offline_blocker: Requires live conversion events across multiple interaction types and attribution windows.
why_not_hard_break: The query returns valid data; metric semantics shift with reporting parameters.

drift_title: Browser event wins dedup and CAPI count is deducted
silent_success_surface: Both Insight Tag and CAPI events can appear in conversion breakdowns.
behavior_change: When both paths send the same eventId, campaign reporting counts only the Insight Tag conversion and deducts the CAPI event.
offline_blocker: Requires live LinkedIn conversion rules, tag traffic, CAPI traffic, and campaign reporting.
why_not_hard_break: Both event paths can be healthy; reporting allocation changes via dedup semantics.
notes: Distinct from existing TikTok event dedup case.

drift_title: Modeled conversions appear in normal columns and Unallocated rows
silent_success_surface: Reports continue returning purchase/detail-page-view conversion columns.
behavior_change: Modeled conversions can be mixed into regular columns, and unallocatable estimates can appear as Unallocated or -20 dimension rows.
offline_blocker: Requires eligible live Sponsored Display reporting with modeled conversions.
why_not_hard_break: The report API succeeds; row allocation and conversion composition change silently.

drift_title: Accepted mail send can be silently suppressed after 202
silent_success_surface: HTTP 202 Accepted from Mail Send has no per-recipient delivery result in the synchronous response.
behavior_change: Suppression lists, bounces, spam reports, or group suppressions can prevent delivery after request acceptance.
offline_blocker: Need live account/event activity tying accepted message IDs to later suppression.
why_not_hard_break: Endpoint contract remains valid; async suppression checks change delivery outcome.

drift_title: SendEmail success can later become bounce, complaint, or delivery delay
silent_success_surface: SendEmail returns a MessageId after accepting the request for sending.
behavior_change: Mailbox-provider outcomes are asynchronous and can later emit bounce, complaint, reject, delivery delay, or delivery events.
offline_blocker: Need live SNS/EventBridge logs to connect accepted MessageIds to later outcomes.
why_not_hard_break: The send API accepts valid requests; delivery result is intentionally asynchronous.

drift_title: Bulk profile import can accept a job while dropping individual profiles
silent_success_surface: Bulk import job creation returns success/accepted and processing continues asynchronously.
behavior_change: Some profiles can be dropped or reported in job errors after the job itself is accepted.
offline_blocker: Need live job results/errors for a real import.
why_not_hard_break: The bulk endpoint accepts valid jobs; failures are per-profile async results.

drift_title: Bulk suppression accepts jobs that later change marketing eligibility
silent_success_surface: Bulk suppression job returns 202 Success.
behavior_change: Suppressed profiles cannot receive email marketing regardless of consent, and missing emails can create immediately suppressed profiles.
offline_blocker: Need live job status and profile state to prove post-acceptance suppression effects.
why_not_hard_break: The API accepts the suppression request; downstream send eligibility changes silently.

drift_title: Users track success may include non-fatal per-object errors
silent_success_surface: Response message can be success while errors array contains non-fatal object errors.
behavior_change: Braze can process valid attributes/events/purchases while skipping invalid objects in the same request.
offline_blocker: Need live response bodies and later profile/event state for exact skipped objects.
why_not_hard_break: The request is not rejected as a whole; individual objects fail.

drift_title: Primary identifier can create duplicate profile despite matching email or phone
silent_success_surface: A valid users/track request can return success while resolving by primary identifier only.
behavior_change: Unknown primary identifiers can create duplicate profiles even when email or phone matches an existing profile.
offline_blocker: Need workspace profile exports before/after to prove duplicate creation.
why_not_hard_break: The API accepts the call; identity resolution outcome changes data shape silently.

drift_title: Create-then-send race can accept data update but miss API-only send target
silent_success_surface: users/track queues profile creation, then messages/send can be called before profile data propagates.
behavior_change: Immediate follow-up sends can race profile propagation and silently miss intended recipients.
offline_blocker: Need timed live calls to prove propagation delay and missed send.
why_not_hard_break: Both endpoints remain valid; the failure is an accepted async sequencing condition.

drift_title: Email prioritization ties retry then do not send
silent_success_surface: Triggered campaign request can be accepted while recipient resolution is retried asynchronously.
behavior_change: Email-prioritization ties or no-profile matches can result in no send after retries.
offline_blocker: Need live duplicate-email profiles and campaign status evidence.
why_not_hard_break: Endpoint contract is unchanged; recipient-level resolution prevents delivery after accepted trigger.

drift_title: Transactional send status progresses from sent to processed, aborted, delivered, or bounced
silent_success_surface: Initial transactional send can produce a dispatch_id while final outcome is reported later.
behavior_change: sent only means dispatched to Braze email partner; later status can abort, deliver, or bounce.
offline_blocker: Need postback/event capture tied to dispatch_id for live proof.
why_not_hard_break: The API accepts the send; final deliverability is asynchronous and recipient-specific.

drift_title: Transactional API 200 OK queues delivery but later status can fail or suppress
silent_success_surface: Successful transactional request returns 200 OK with delivery_id and queued_at.
behavior_change: Queued delivery can later become failed, undeliverable, bounced, or suppressed.
offline_blocker: Need delivery logs/webhook/reporting data for the delivery_id.
why_not_hard_break: The send request succeeds; downstream delivery and suppression status changes after queueing.

drift_title: Directory objects degrade to id/type with null properties without resource-specific read permissions
silent_success_surface: 200 OK collection still returned.
behavior_change: Members of object types the app cannot read are present but only @odata.type and id are populated; other properties are null.
offline_blocker: Requires tenant objects with mixed member types and controlled Graph app permissions.
why_not_hard_break: The relationship query succeeds and returns objects; payload fidelity changes by permission.

drift_title: memberOf returns null group properties when token lacks directory/group read breadth
silent_success_surface: 200 OK with membership rows.
behavior_change: Membership entries can show id and type while displayName, mail, groupTypes, and other fields are null.
offline_blocker: Needs Entra tenant, user memberships, and deliberately under-scoped app token.
why_not_hard_break: No auth failure is required; caller receives a valid response with redacted fields.

api_surface: GraphQL query fields for protected resources
drift_title: Unauthorized and nonexistent resources both resolve as null without errors
silent_success_surface: GraphQL 200 response with null field.
behavior_change: Query fields can return null when the caller lacks permission, intentionally matching nonexistent-resource behavior.
offline_blocker: Needs GitLab project/resource matrix with known inaccessible and nonexistent IDs.
why_not_hard_break: GraphQL request succeeds and no errors array entry is required for the authorization null.

api_surface: User objects across pages, blocks, rich text, people properties
drift_title: User objects collapse to minimal id/object without user-information capability
silent_success_surface: Content APIs still return objects containing user references.
behavior_change: User references always include object and id, while name, avatar_url, type, and person details may be omitted by connection capability.
offline_blocker: Requires Notion connections with different user capability levels and shared pages containing user references.
why_not_hard_break: Content retrieval succeeds; embedded user payloads are reduced rather than rejected.

drift_title: Person email only appears with email-address user capability
silent_success_surface: 200 OK user/list response when the endpoint is allowed.
behavior_change: person.email is present only when the connection has the capability for email address access.
offline_blocker: Requires separate Notion tokens/capabilities with and without email access.
why_not_hard_break: The user object remains usable; only the email subfield is absent.

drift_title: Guest participant emails return empty strings under email address display rules
silent_success_surface: 200 OK reports and dashboard responses.
behavior_change: email, participant_email, or user_email values are empty for guest/external/anonymous participants unless exceptions apply.
offline_blocker: Requires past meetings with host-account users and guest/external participants.
why_not_hard_break: Affected APIs continue returning participant rows with empty email fields.

drift_title: Private event visibility hides event details from non-attendee readers
silent_success_surface: Calendar/event reads can still return availability or reduced event representations.
behavior_change: Private event details are suppressed by attendee status and ACL visibility even when calendar access succeeds.
offline_blocker: Requires calendars with ACL variations and private/default/public visibility cases.
why_not_hard_break: Calendar access can succeed while event details are suppressed by visibility rules.

platform: Apple Sign in with Apple JS
drift_title: Name and email user object returned only on first authorization
silent_success_surface: Subsequent sign-ins still authenticate successfully.
behavior_change: Apple returns the user object containing name and email only the first time the user authorizes the app.
offline_blocker: Requires Sign in with Apple app/client setup and repeated authorization lifecycle testing.
why_not_hard_break: Authentication succeeds and stable user identifier remains; contact payload is one-time/conditional.

drift_title: Privacy thresholds zero out granular engagement metrics
silent_success_surface: 200 OK analytics-style responses.
behavior_change: Impression, engagement, and click metrics can be zeroed when privacy thresholds are not met.
offline_blocker: Requires approved LinkedIn Marketing API access and low-volume segments below thresholds.
why_not_hard_break: Reports return successfully; values are privacy-protected by thresholding.

drift_title: Public user email becomes null when no public profile email is set
silent_success_surface: 200 OK user object.
behavior_change: The email key remains in the response but is null unless the user selected a publicly visible primary email.
offline_blocker: Needs accounts with different public email visibility settings.
why_not_hard_break: Lookup succeeds and schema remains stable; field value is redacted to null.
notes: Field-redaction candidate, distinct from existing Slack email case.

drift_title: HTTP 200 list response may still contain invalid XML
silent_success_surface: 200 OK from ListObjectsV2.
behavior_change: Clients that equate HTTP success with parseable object listings can skip, truncate, or mishandle listings.
offline_blocker: Requires observing live S3 service responses and parser behavior.
why_not_hard_break: The request succeeds at HTTP level; only response-content assumptions fail.

platform: AWS S3 Express One Zone / directory buckets
drift_title: Directory bucket listings are not lexicographically ordered
silent_success_surface: Successful paginated object listing.
behavior_change: Resume, shard-boundary, or completeness logic based on lexicographic key order can produce omissions or duplicates.
offline_blocker: Requires live directory-bucket listing behavior.
why_not_hard_break: The API returns 200 and object entries; the semantic drift is ordering.

drift_title: Object event notifications are at-least-once and may be duplicate or out of order
silent_success_surface: Notification delivery to SQS/SNS/Lambda/EventBridge.
behavior_change: Consumers assuming exactly-once ordered events can double-process objects or apply stale state.
offline_blocker: Duplicate/out-of-order delivery is service timing dependent.
why_not_hard_break: Notifications are still delivered; correctness depends on idempotency and sequencing logic.

drift_title: Access grants/revokes and bucket recreation are eventually consistent
silent_success_surface: Successful IAM or bucket delete/create operation.
behavior_change: Revoked principals can retain access briefly and recreated buckets can remain inaccessible for minutes.
offline_blocker: Requires live propagation timing across Google Cloud control planes.
why_not_hard_break: The operations complete successfully; the drift is delayed effect.

drift_title: Public cacheable objects may serve stale content after update or delete
silent_success_surface: Successful object update/delete plus successful cached GET.
behavior_change: Clients can receive old bytes or metadata for publicly readable cached objects until cache lifetime expires.
offline_blocker: Requires live cache path and object metadata/cache-control behavior.
why_not_hard_break: Reads still succeed; stale visibility is allowed by cache semantics.

drift_title: 2xx/202 marks delivery complete and batched webhooks are all-or-none
silent_success_surface: Webhook endpoint returns 200-204, including 202 Accepted.
behavior_change: Returning success before durable per-event processing can lose events locally; batched delivery cannot partially acknowledge items.
offline_blocker: Requires live Event Grid retry/dead-letter behavior and receiver timing.
why_not_hard_break: Delivery contract functions; application-level processing assumptions are fragile.

api_surface: SQS event source partial batch responses
drift_title: Empty or null batchItemFailures is treated as complete batch success
silent_success_surface: Successful Lambda invocation response.
behavior_change: A handler returning empty/null failure list after per-record errors causes Lambda to delete the whole batch.
offline_blocker: Requires live event source mapping semantics for message deletion/retry confirmation.
why_not_hard_break: The Lambda invocation succeeds; per-record failure signaling is semantically wrong.

drift_title: Push subscriptions acknowledge by HTTP response without exactly-once confirmation
silent_success_surface: Successful push endpoint HTTP response.
behavior_change: Push consumers can return success without knowing whether Pub/Sub processed the acknowledgment; exactly-once applies only to pull subscriptions.
offline_blocker: Requires live Pub/Sub push delivery and acknowledgment timing.
why_not_hard_break: Messages still deliver; guarantee level and ack observability drift.

drift_title: changes[] may be incomplete whenever nextPageToken is present
silent_success_surface: Successful changes.list response.
behavior_change: Sync loops that process only the first successful page can miss file changes; newStartPageToken arrives only at the end.
offline_blocker: Requires live Drive change streams and page-token progression.
why_not_hard_break: The API returns 200 with valid changes; clients must continue pagination.

drift_title: Drive push notifications are empty hints with non-sequential message numbers
silent_success_surface: Successful HTTPS notification POST and receiver 2xx/102 response.
behavior_change: Receivers expecting contiguous offsets or payload content can skip reconciliation or mis-detect gaps.
offline_blocker: Requires live notification channels and timing.
why_not_hard_break: Notification delivery succeeds; semantics require follow-up API reads and tolerance for non-contiguous numbering.

drift_title: Auto-update deployment advances to new default model version
silent_success_surface: Same deployment name and API route continue to return 200 after Azure changes the default model version.
behavior_change: Applications can see changed reasoning, compatibility, safety, or output format after automatic model version upgrade.
offline_blocker: Needs Azure resource deployment metadata or version upgrade history.
why_not_hard_break: Deployment remains callable; behavior changes rather than endpoint failure.

drift_title: Claude aliases migrate to newest dated snapshot
silent_success_surface: Requests using model aliases continue to succeed as alias target changes.
behavior_change: Quality, refusals, tool behavior, and formatting can change when an alias resolves to a newer snapshot.
offline_blocker: Need live alias resolution or vendor model list over time.
why_not_hard_break: Alias remains valid; vendor recommends pinned dated ids for consistent production behavior.

drift_title: Auto-updated Gemini stable/latest aliases change target version
silent_success_surface: Same Gemini model alias succeeds while the underlying stable version advances.
behavior_change: Generated text, reasoning defaults, token behavior, and safety behavior may shift when alias target changes.
offline_blocker: Requires live Gemini model metadata or longitudinal prompt comparison.
why_not_hard_break: Alias remains accepted; docs advise specific stable versions for production consistency.
notes: Distinct from existing Gemini API key scope case.

drift_title: omni-moderation-latest classifier alias can alter category scores
silent_success_surface: Moderation calls return normal results while flags/category_scores may shift across latest-model updates.
behavior_change: Application thresholds can be crossed by classifier alias/model updates without response schema failure.
offline_blocker: Requires live classifier snapshots and threshold regression corpus.
why_not_hard_break: API returns 200 with the same response schema; downstream filtering changes silently.
notes: Official OpenAI docs spot-checked during collection.

drift_title: Gemini safety filters produce successful blocked responses
silent_success_surface: GenerateContent may return a structured response with finishReason SAFETY instead of requested content.
behavior_change: Content that previously returned text can be blocked or redacted by model/safety classifier changes.
offline_blocker: Needs live prompts and safetyRatings comparison across model versions/settings.
why_not_hard_break: Request is syntactically valid and response is structured; absence of content is policy/filter behavior.

drift_title: Streaming classifier can end with stop_reason refusal
silent_success_surface: Streaming response starts successfully, then ends with stop_reason=refusal.
behavior_change: A prompt may yield partial output or refusal termination as safety classifiers intervene during streaming.
offline_blocker: Requires live Claude streaming prompts and classifier-triggering cases.
why_not_hard_break: Transport and API call succeed; caller must interpret stop_reason rather than catch an error.

drift_title: Index writes acknowledged before searchable state
silent_success_surface: Add/update record returns taskID while search results can lag until task completion.
behavior_change: Fresh records may be absent or stale in search/ranking until asynchronous indexing finishes.
offline_blocker: Requires live index task timing and search probes before/after waitTask.
why_not_hard_break: Write request succeeds by design; visibility delay is asynchronous processing behavior.

drift_title: Geocoder returns partial_match for accepted address query
silent_success_surface: Geocoding request returns OK and coordinates, but result may only partially match the original address.
behavior_change: Incomplete or misspelled inputs can resolve to alternative addresses or approximate matches without request failure.
offline_blocker: Requires live geocoder data and address corpus because map data and matching evolve.
why_not_hard_break: The call succeeds with results; quality signal is in partial_match rather than an error.

drift_title: Geocoder data updates and match_code confidence alter accepted results
silent_success_surface: Forward geocode returns features while match_code/confidence or coordinates may change as data improves.
behavior_change: The same query can move between confidence levels or coordinates as source data and matching change.
offline_blocker: Requires live Mapbox geocoding data and repeat probes over time.
why_not_hard_break: API returns a successful feature list; drift is in match confidence, ranking, and coordinates.

drift_title: Default published comment listing silently omits held/spam comments
silent_success_surface: commentThreads.list returns 200 with default moderationStatus=published while heldForReview/likelySpam are excluded.
behavior_change: Comments can disappear from default API reads when moderation classifiers or channel settings hold them.
offline_blocker: Requires channel-owner auth and live comments moving through YouTube moderation states.
why_not_hard_break: The list call succeeds; caller must request moderation states to see held/spam queues.

drift_title: Page retrieval silently truncates high-cardinality property references
silent_success_surface: HTTP 200 page object is returned while relation/people/title/rich_text references beyond documented limits may be missing or degraded.
behavior_change: Retrieve page is not a complete read for high-cardinality properties; clients need page property item retrieval.
offline_blocker: Needs live workspace data with more than 25 references.
why_not_hard_break: The endpoint succeeds and returns a page object; only completeness semantics drift.

drift_title: Rollup property pagination returns interim aggregate semantics
silent_success_surface: HTTP 200 list response includes rollup data and next_cursor, but aggregate values can represent only the traversed subset until has_more is false.
behavior_change: Large rollups paginate and concurrent underlying changes can affect final computed values.
offline_blocker: Requires live rollup with enough related pages/properties to paginate.
why_not_hard_break: Requests succeed; the drift is when a returned aggregate is final.

drift_title: Data source query paginates only through 10,000 matching results
silent_success_surface: Query responses continue returning list pages, but pagination stops at the documented 10000-result limit for a single query.
behavior_change: Large data sources require partitioned queries; a normal cursor loop can finish successfully while omitting matching pages beyond the cap.
offline_blocker: Needs a live data source with more than 10000 matching pages.
why_not_hard_break: No endpoint failure is required; the result set is bounded by query semantics.

drift_title: users query without limit defaults to 200 users in 2026-07+
silent_success_surface: GraphQL query succeeds but returns only 200 matching users when limit is omitted.
behavior_change: Integrations that omit limit/page silently under-sync users after targeting newer API versions.
offline_blocker: Needs a monday account with more than 200 users and versioned API header.
why_not_hard_break: The query resolves successfully; response is a valid first page rather than a failure.

drift_title: updates query maxes at 100 updates per page in 2025-01+
silent_success_surface: The updates query returns successful data but only up to 100 updates per page for newer API versions.
behavior_change: Clients expecting a single updates call to cover all updates silently miss older updates unless they paginate.
offline_blocker: Requires a board/account with more than 100 matching updates under API version 2025-01+.
why_not_hard_break: No endpoint or field is removed; the server returns a valid bounded page.

drift_title: ITEM_PREVIEW user events no longer emitted for collaborators
silent_success_surface: Event stream polling succeeds, but preview events are created only for content owners rather than all collaborators.
behavior_change: Collaborator-level preview analytics lose collaborator preview signals while owner preview visibility remains.
offline_blocker: Needs a live Box file with multiple collaborators and event polling before/after preview.
why_not_hard_break: Event API consumption continues normally; emitted event population changes.

api_surface: Create Request endpoint for anonymous requests
drift_title: Anonymous request creation inserts email verification and suspended-ticket workflow
silent_success_surface: Create Request can still create an anonymous request, but the ticket is suspended until requester email verification completes.
behavior_change: Automations assuming anonymous requests immediately become normal tickets can see delayed or held tickets instead.
offline_blocker: Needs a live Zendesk account accepting anonymous requests to observe suspension and verification state.
why_not_hard_break: The endpoint remains available and creates workflow state; lifecycle changes after successful submission.

drift_title: Conversation retrieval returns only the 500 most recent conversation parts
silent_success_surface: Retrieve conversation succeeds and returns a Conversation model while long conversations are capped to the 500 most recent parts.
behavior_change: Archival/export clients can treat a successful conversation payload as complete while missing older conversation parts.
offline_blocker: Requires a live Intercom conversation with more than 500 parts.
why_not_hard_break: The API returns HTTP 200 with a valid conversation object; only history completeness drifts.
notes: Official reference plus live-proof blocker makes this S4-ready.
